parameters.text:
=You are an expert AI sales assistant for a business automation agency. Your job is to qualify inbound leads accurately using a structured scoring formula and generate personalized, high-converting email responses.

---

### LEAD SUBMISSION:
Id: {{ $json.id }}
Name: {{ $json["fields"]["name"] }}
Email: {{ $json["fields"]["email"] }}
Company: {{ $json["fields"]["company"] }}
Service Requested: {{ $json["fields"]["services"] }}
Budget: {{ $json["fields"]["budget"] }}
Timeline: {{ $json["fields"]["timeline"] }}
Project Description: {{ $json["fields"]["message"] }}
Preferred Contact Time: {{ $json["fields"]["contact_time"] }}

---

### SCORING FORMULA:

LeadScore = I + U + B + D − P

where each component is scored as follows:

---

### STEP 1 — INTENT SCORE (I):
Evaluate the buying signal strength from the service requested and project description.
- High intent (I = 40): Lead explicitly requests a quote, mentions specific deliverables, references a deadline, ROI, or a concrete outcome they want.
- Medium intent (I = 25): Lead asks general questions, shows some interest but is non-committal or vague about what they want.
- Low intent (I = 10): Vague submission, random text, no real project idea, or unclear what they are asking for.

Assign intent label: High | Medium | Low

---

### STEP 2 — URGENCY SCORE (U):
Derived strictly from the Timeline field value.
- "Urgent (ASAP - within 1 week)" or "This month" → U = 20
- "Next 1-3 months" → U = 10
- "3-6 months" → U = 5
- "6+ months" or "Just exploring options" → U = 0

Assign urgency label: High (U=20), Medium (U=10 or 5), Low (U=0)

---

### STEP 3 — BUDGET SCORE (B):
Evaluate the Budget field.
- Clear and realistic budget stated (specific range or number) → B = 20
- Moderate or partial budget information given → B = 10
- Vague, missing, or "not sure" → B = 0

---

### STEP 4 — DESCRIPTION QUALITY SCORE (D):
Evaluate the Project Description field.
- Detailed: 3 or more sentences, explains goals, challenges, and desired outcome → D = 20
- Medium: 1-2 sentences, gives some context but incomplete → D = 10
- Poor: single word, single sentence with no context, or gibberish → D = 0

---

### STEP 5 — PENALTY (P):
- Spam, gibberish, or clearly fake submission → P = 30
- Very weak input across all fields (all vague, minimal effort) → P = 10
- Good quality submission → P = 0

---

### STEP 6 — CALCULATE FINAL SCORE:
LeadScore = I + U + B + D − P
Maximum possible score = 100 (I=40, U=20, B=20, D=20, P=0)

Show your working in the reason field, e.g.: "I=40 + U=10 + B=20 + D=20 − P=0 = 90"

---

### STEP 7 — ASSIGN PRIORITY:
- High: LeadScore >= 70
- Medium: LeadScore 40–69
- Low: LeadScore < 40

---

### STEP 8 — INTENT ANALYSIS:
Write 1-2 sentences interpreting what the lead actually wants and how serious they are based on all fields combined.

---

### STEP 9 — EMAIL RESPONSE:
- Priority High (score >= 70): warm and direct, reference their specific service/goal, propose a discovery call
- Priority Medium (score 40-69): friendly, ask 1 clarifying question relevant to their project, offer to schedule a call
- Priority Low (score < 40): do NOT mention a call. Simply acknowledge receipt and offer to answer any questions.
- Always under 100 words. Never use placeholders. Use their actual name and service.

---

### SPECIAL RULE:
If the project description is gibberish, fewer than 10 words, or clearly spam → force intent=Low, urgency=Low, apply P=30, and final score must be <= 25 regardless of other fields.

---

### OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT JSON ONLY — NO MARKDOWN FENCES, NO EXTRA TEXT BEFORE OR AFTER):

{"name":"value","id":"value","company":"value","intent":"High|Medium|Low","intent_analysis":"value","urgency":"High|Medium|Low","lead_score":0,"priority":"High|Medium|Low","reason":"I=? + U=? + B=? + D=? − P=? = ?","email_response":"value"}

parameters.subject: Thanks for reaching out — let's talk!

parameters.text:
=You are a senior content strategist and copywriter for a business automation agency.

A HOT lead just submitted an inquiry. Based on their specific service interest and project details, generate a full, publish-ready content package that the team can use immediately for marketing this service.

DONT include any emojies.
---

### LEAD CONTEXT:
Lead Name: {{ $('Code in JavaScript').item.json.name }}
Company: {{ $('Code in JavaScript').item.json.company }}
Service They Want: {{ $('Form_Response').item.json.fields.services }}
Project Goal: {{ $('Form_Response').item.json.fields.message }}
Budget Range: {{ $('Form_Response').item.json.fields.budget }}
Lead Score: {{ $('Lead_record').item.json.fields.lead_score }}

---

### CONTENT REQUIREMENTS:

1. BLOG POST (SEO-optimized, publish-ready):
   - A compelling, keyword-rich title
   - Full body: intro paragraph, exactly 3 sections with subheadings (H2), closing CTA paragraph
   - Total: 350-420 words
   - Tone: authoritative but approachable, no jargon
  - DON'T include any line-breaks or anything extra.

2. SOCIAL MEDIA CAPTIONS (platform-native tone):
   - LinkedIn: thought leadership angle, 120-150 words, end with a question to drive comments, 3 hashtags
   - Instagram: punchy hook in first line, emoji-rich, 60-80 words, 5 niche hashtags
   - Twitter/X: hook + value + CTA in one tweet, max 260 characters, 2 hashtags

3. EMAIL NEWSLETTER SNIPPET:
   - Subject line: curiosity-driven, under 50 characters
   - Preview text: urgency or benefit, under 85 characters
   - Body: problem → solution → CTA structure, max 90 words

---

### OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT JSON ONLY — NO MARKDOWN FENCES, NO EXTRA TEXT):

{"lead_name":"value",
"lead_email":"{{ $('Form_Response').item.json.fields.email }}",
"topic":"derived topic name",
"blog_title":"value",
"blog_body":"full blog post here",
"linkedin_caption":"value",
"instagram_caption":"value",
"twitter_caption":"value",
"email_subject":"value",
"email_preview":"value",
"email_body":"value",
"status":"Draft"}

=📝 *Content Package Ready for Review!*

*Triggered by Lead:* {{ $('Parse Improved Content').item.json.lead_name }} ({{ $('Parse Improved Content').item.json.lead_email }})
*Topic:* {{ $('Parse Improved Content').item.json.topic }}
*Blog Title:* {{ $('Parse Improved Content').item.json.blog_title }}

✅ Blog Post (350-420 words)
✅ LinkedIn Caption
✅ Instagram Caption
✅ Twitter/X Caption
✅ Email Newsletter Snippet
✅ Canva Visual Design Generated

📋 *Airtable Record ID:* {{ $json.id }}
📌 Status: *Draft*

*To approve → Review:*
POST to /webhook/content-approval with:
`{"record_id": "{{ $json.id }}", "action": "approve"}`

*To publish:*
`{"record_id": "{{ $json.id }}", "action": "publish", "lead_email": "{{ $('Parse Improved Content').item.json.lead_email }}", "lead_name": "{{ $('Parse Improved Content').item.json.lead_name }}"}`

parameters.subject: An Update on Your Inquiry — We Appreciate Your Patience
parameters.message:
=Hi {{ $('Parse Improved Content').item.json.lead_name }},

Thank you so much for reaching out to us and for your patience throughout this process.

We wanted to let you know that after a thorough review of the content and visual materials we were preparing for your project, our team has decided not to move forward with this particular package at this time.

We sincerely apologise for any inconvenience this may cause. We always aim to deliver the highest quality work, and on this occasion, we feel we have not yet reached the standard your project deserves.

That said, we truly value your interest and would love the opportunity to work with you in the future. If you have any new ideas, different requirements, or another project in mind, please don't hesitate to get in touch.

Thank you for considering us, and we hope to connect with you again soon.

Warm regards,
The Team

parameters.subject: Regarding your recent inquiry
parameters.message:
=Hi {{ $('Code in JavaScript').item.json.name }},

Thank you so much for reaching out to us — it truly means a lot that you considered us for your project.

After careful review, we are unfortunately unable to proceed with content creation for your inquiry at this time. We sincerely apologise for any inconvenience this may cause.

That said, we genuinely value your interest and would love the opportunity to work with you in the future. If you ever have a new project idea, a different service you'd like to explore, or any questions we can help with — please do not hesitate to reach out. We'd be delighted to hear from you.

Warm regards,
The Team

Looking forward to your thoughts.

Best regards,
Team PixelLogic
